publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-20 23:59:06 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes lecture notes, exams and original manuscript of his book, Biological Sciences (1967).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William Keeton papers, #21-1-3355. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Keeton received his undergraduate degree from the University of Chicago; his M.S. from Virginia Polytechnic Institute (1956) and a Ph.D. from Cornell University (1958). He was an internationally known authority on bird orientation and migration and his research spanned the fields of entomology, taxonomy, evolutionary theory, and the homing behavior of pigeons.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also the Keeton pigeon orientation database: https://doi.org/10.7298/aq7r-sg91

did.unittitle: Biology 109, Lecture I Introduction and Evolution
First Year, Graduate Exams. (NB and B)
Series IV. "Biological Science" (Textbook), Manuscript and Galleys
